methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Salient Motion is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $12M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a peer universe of 9 comparables in robotics and manufacturing—1 public company and 8 private firms—including later-stage reference points such as 1X Technologies ($10.0B Series B valuation), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M).

Two methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage advancement, using the $12M total raised as a proxy base, yielding $24M. Peer-median analysis across the 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range of $300M to $10.0B. The peer set exhibits substantial dispersion, reflecting heterogeneity in product maturity and market penetration within the sector.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $20M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $550M. This wide spread reflects material uncertainty about Salient Motion's competitive positioning relative to the peer set and the extent to which later-stage multiples compress at Series A. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively; peer-median valuation dominates the output given the 6-company sample size. Key uncertainties include product differentiation, addressable market traction, and whether company trajectories will converge toward lower-quartile peers ($300M–$500M) or mid-range performers ($1.0B–$1.8B).
